About: It is about testing the working ability and instinct of small, often short-legged terriers or Dachshunds.
It is about human-made tunnels that dogs must navigate while scenting a rat.
It is about the controlled hunting encounter where neither the dog nor the quarry are endangered.
Affects: It affects the working ability and instinct of terriers and Dachshunds.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Canadian Kennel Club.
It is associated with the American Kennel Club.
It is associated with the American Working Terrier Association.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the sport of dog training and testing.
BuiltIn: It was built in human-made tunnels.
ConsistsOf: It consists of scenting a rat.
It consists of barking, scratching, staring, pawing, or digging.
CreatedFor: It was created for testing the working ability and instinct of terriers and Dachshunds.
CreatedTo: It was created to test the working ability and instinct of terriers and Dachshunds.
It was created to introduce dogs to den work and quarry.
It was created to gauge a dog's natural hunting aptitude.
HasA: It has levels of increasing difficulty.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by human-made tunnels.
It is characterized by the use of rats as quarry.
It is characterized by specific breeds of terriers and Dachshunds.
It is characterized by different levels of difficulty.
It is characterized by the need for dogs to follow a scent trail.
It is characterized by the dog working the quarry.
It is characterized by the quarry being protected by wooden bars.
HeldIn: It was held in the United States.
It was held in the European Union.
IsA: It is a test of working ability and instinct.
It is a test for small, often short-legged terriers or Dachshunds.
It is a test involving human-made tunnels.
It is a controlled hunting encounter.
It is a program broken down into several levels of increasing difficulty.
KnownAs: It is known as an earthdog test.
KnownFor: It is known for testing the working ability and instinct of terriers and Dachshunds.
It is known for its use of human-made tunnels.
It is known for involving quarry such as rats.
It is known for its different levels of difficulty and titles.
MemberOf: It is a member of activities sanctioned by the Canadian Kennel Club.
It is a member of activities sanctioned by the American Kennel Club.
It is a member of activities sanctioned by the American Working Terrier Association.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
It occurred in the European Union.
PartOf: It is part of the American Kennel Club's program.
It is part of the American Working Terrier Association's den trials.
PerformedBy: It was performed by terriers and Dachshunds.
It was performed by dogs registered with the AKC.
It was performed by purebred dogs with ILP numbers.
It was performed by two dogs hunting together as a brace in the Master Earthdog test.
UsedAs: It is used as a way to gauge a dog's natural hunting aptitude.
It is used as a noncompetitive venue.
It is used as a test for working ability and instinct.
UsedFor: It is used for testing the working ability and instinct of terriers and Dachshunds.
It is used for introducing dogs to den work and quarry.
It is used for demonstrating the consistency of a hunting earthdog.
It is used for promoting and testing the working abilities of terriers and dachshunds.
UsedIn: It is used in AKC earthdog tests.
It is used in AWTA earthdog den trials.
UsedTo: It is used to test the working ability and instinct of small terriers or Dachshunds.
It is used to introduce dogs to den work and quarry.
It is used to demonstrate a dog's willingness to perform required tasks.
It is used to gauge a dog's natural hunting aptitude.
It is used to demonstrate the consistency of a hunting earthdog.
